mechanism_narrative: An expanding industrial production index signals rising manufacturing output and corporate revenue growth, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if 20-day forward returns following month-over-month INDPRO increases are not positive in at least 55% of occurrences, or if the Rank IC is negative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate signals weakening labor demand and impending earnings compression, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 55% of instances when UNRATE has risen more than 0.3pp over the prior 3 months, or if the signal Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Jointly elevated federal funds rates and contracting industrial production create a dual drag from restrictive financing costs and falling output, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS exceeds 4 percent and INDPRO declines month-over-month
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if 60-day forward returns following joint high-FEDFUNDS and falling-INDPRO months are positive in more than 50% of instances, or if the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) in a pooled regression.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recoveries, an acceleration in retail sales signals reviving consumer demand and inventory restocking, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if 5-day forward returns following accelerating RSAFS are not significantly higher in post-shock recovery periods than in normal expansions (t-test p > 0.05), or if returns are negative in more than 45% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in housing starts signals residential investment momentum and spillover demand for construction materials and consumer durables, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if month-over-month increases in HOUST are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in fewer than 55% of historical instances, or if the walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in industrial production signals acute supply-side disruption and negative operating leverage, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines more than 1.0 percent month-over-month
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if 5-day forward returns following month-over-month INDPRO declines greater than 1.0% are negative in fewer than 55% of instances, or if the mean return in those periods is not statistically below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the consumer price index signals rising input costs and forward earnings compression through margin pressure, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if month-over-month CPIAUCSL increases are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in fewer than 55% of instances, or if the Rank IC turns positive over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a marked slowdown in nonfarm payroll growth signals saturated labor markets and impending earnings downgrades, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if 60-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 55% of late-cycle instances when PAYEMS 3-month growth drops below 300k, or if the signal underperforms the unconditional mean (Sharpe < 0) over any 5-year window.
